Moreover, under a mild temporal monotonicity assumption on the solution, we extend this nonexistence result to the case involving a nonlocal memory term, that is, $\gamma>0.$ On the other hand, in the absence of the Hardy potential, we establish local-in-time existence and uniqueness of mild solutions in $L^\infty(\mathbbm{H}^N)$ by means of a fixed-point argument. entries.lean_statement:
theorem thm_22
  {alpha : Type*} [MeasurableSpace alpha] [TopologicalSpace alpha] [T2Space alpha] [Fintype alpha]
  (u v : ℝ → alpha → ℝ) (T T_max T_eps eps : ℝ) (lam mu p q gamma alpha_par : ℝ)
  (f : ℝ → alpha → ℝ) (u_0 psi : alpha → ℝ) (Γ_gamma : ℝ) (h : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ)
  (hT_eps : T_eps ≤ C * eps ^ ((beta - (gamma + p) / (p - 1) - alpha_par)⁻1))
  (hT_max : IsFinite T_max) (hH_eps : ∀ x, psi x ≤ c)
  (hp : 1 < p ∧ p < (mu + 2) / mu) (hgamma : 0 ≤ gamma ∧ gamma < 1) (halpha : -1 < alpha_par)
  : ∃! u_sol : ℝ → alpha → ℝ,
    (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T_max, ContinuousOn u_sol (Set.Icc 0 t)) ∧
    (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T_max, ContinuousOn (fun x => (u_sol t x) ^ p) (Set.Icc 0 t)) ∧
    (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T_max, u_sol t ∈ Lp (alpha := alpha) (mu := volume) (fun x => (u_sol t x) ^ q)) ∧
    (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T_max, u_sol t ∈ Lp (alpha := alpha) (mu := volume) (fun x => u_sol t x))

entries.lean_statement:
theorem life
  {gamma alpha : ℝ} {p Q : ℝ} {mu : ℝ → ℝ} {eps beta : ℝ} {u0 : ℝ} {f : ℝ → ℝ} {T_eps C : ℝ}
  (hgamma : gamma ∈ Set.Icc 0 1)
  (halpha : alpha ∈ Set.Ioo (-1) 0)
  (hp : p ∈ Set.Ioo 1 ((Q + 2 * gamma - (mu lam) - 2 * alpha) / (Q - 2 - (mu lam) - 2 * alpha)))
  (hu0 : u0 ∈ Set.Ici 0)
  (hf_int : 0 < ∫ ξ, ‖ξ‖_H ^ (-(mu lam)) * f ξ ∧ ∫ ξ, ‖ξ‖_H ^ (-(mu lam)) * f ξ < infty)
  (hf_lower : ∀ ξ, ‖ξ‖_H ≥ 1 → f ξ ≥ eps * ‖ξ‖_H ^ (-2 * beta))
  (hbeta : beta ∈ Set.Ioo (Q / 2 - (mu lam) / 2) ((gamma + p) / (p - 1) + alpha))
  (heps : eps > 0)
  (hT_eps : T_eps > 0)
  (hC : C > 0) :
  T_eps ≤ C * eps ^ ((beta - (gamma + p) / (p - 1) - alpha)⁻1)

nearby_claims: Theorem \ref{Thm Blowup O}, we address the question of how rapidly blow-up occurs in the subcritical regime.
